result.markdown:
--- title: "Computer Vision" subtitle: "Is this AI?" author: - "[Tom Jemmett](mailto:thomas.jemmett@nhs.net)" date: 2024-10-10 date-format: "D MMMM YYYY" format: revealjs: theme: [default, ../su_presentation.scss] transition: none chalkboard: buttons: false preview-links: auto slide-number: false auto-animate: true reference-location: document footer: | Learn more about [The Strategy Unit](https://www.strategyunitwm.nhs.uk/) --- ## What is Computer Vision (CV) ::: {.fragment} > Computer vision is a field of **computer science** that focuses on enabling **computers to > identify and understand objects and people** in **images and videos**. ::: ::: {.fragment} > Like other types of AI, computer vision seeks to **perform and automate tasks** that **replicate > human capabilities**. ::: ::: {.fragment} > In this case, computer vision seeks to replicate both the way **humans see**, and > the way humans **make sense** of what they see. ::: ::: footer Source: [What is computer vision? Microsoft](https://azure.microsoft.com/en-gb/resources/ cloud-computing-dictionary/what-is-computer-vision#object-classification) ::: # What tasks can CV be used for? {.center .inverse} --- ## Classification ::: {.fragment fragment-index=6} *Assign a single label to each image* ::: :::: {.columns} ::: {.column width="40%"} ::: {.fragment fragment-index=1} ![](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/EliSchwartz/imagenet-sample-images/refs/heads/master/n02102177_Welsh_springer_spaniel.JPEG){height=300px} ::: ::: {.fragment fragment-index=3} Dog ::: ::: {.fragment fragment-index=4} Welsh Spaniel ::: ::: {.fragment fragment-index=5} Animal in water ::: ::: ::: {.column width="20%"} ::: ::: {.column width="40%"} ::: {.fragment fragment-index=2} ![](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/EliSchwartz/imagenet-sample-images/refs/heads/master/n02102480_Sussex_spaniel.JPEG){height=300px} ::: ::: {.fragment fragment-index=3} Dog ::: ::: {.fragment fragment-index=4} Sussex Spaniel ::: ::: {.fragment fragment-index=5} Animal on land ::: ::: :::: ::: footer Images from [imagenet](https://www.image-net.org/) ::: ## Multi-Classification ::: {.fragment fragment-index=2} *Assign one or more labels to each image* ::: :::: {.columns} ::: {.column width="40%"} ![](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/EliSchwartz/imagenet-sample-images/refs/heads/master/n02102177_Welsh_springer_spaniel.JPEG){height=300px} ::: {.fragment fragment-index=1} Dog, Welsh Spaniel, Animal in water ::: ::: ::: {.column width="20%"} ::: ::: {.column width="40%"} ![](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/EliSchwartz/imagenet-sample-images/refs/heads/master/n02102480_Sussex_spaniel.JPEG){height=300px} ::: {.fragment fragment-index=1} Dog, Sussex Spaniel, Animal on land ::: ::: :::: ::: footer Images from [imagenet](https://www.image-net.org/) ::: ## Object Detection ![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/3/38/Detected-with-YOLO--Schreibtisch-mit-Objekten.jpg/1119px-Detected-with-YOLO--Schreibtisch-mit-Objekten.jpg) ::: footer Image sourced from [Wikimedia](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Detected-with-YOLO--Schreibtisch-mit-Objekten.jpg) ::: ## Event Detection ![](https://www.autonomousvehicleinternational.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/05/AdobeStock_215808766-1024x596.jpeg) ::: footer [Is camera-only the future of self-driving cars?](https://www.autonomousvehicleinternational.com/features/is-camera-only-the-future-of-self-driving-cars.html) ::: ## How does CV work? { .inverse} ### for classification tasks ::: {.incremental} - get a very large corpus or labelled images - convert the images to a form that the computer can work with (tensors) - feed into a convolutional neural network - profit? ::: ## Large corpus of labelled images ### ImageNet * a large visual database * over 14 million hand-annotated images * more than 20,000 categories * each category has "several hundred" images * started in 2006 ## Convert the images to tensors :::: {.columns} ::: {.column width="50%"} A fancy way of saying: turn the images into a 2d table of values between 0 and 1 ::: ::: {.column width="50%"}
::: { .fragment .fade-in-then-out fragment-index=1}
::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](index_files/figure-revealjs/unnamed-chunk-1-1.png){width=480} ::: :::
::: ::: { .fragment .fade-in-then-out fragment-index=2}
::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](index_files/figure-revealjs/unnamed-chunk-2-1.png){width=480} ::: :::
:::
::: :::: ## (Convolutional) Neural Networks {{< video https://youtu.be/aircAruvnKk start="215" width="100%" height="85%" >}} ::: footer [3Blue1Brown, YouTube Channel](https://www.youtube.com/c/3blue1brown) ::: ## Use pre-trained models Models that have been pre-trained on some image datasets which can be downloaded and used ::: { .incremental } * [ResNet](https://huggingface.co/microsoft/resnet-50) (Microsoft Research) * [Inception](https://huggingface.co/docs/timm/en/models/inception-v4) (Google) * [Trending classifiers from Hugging Face](https://huggingface.co/models?pipeline_tag=image-classification&sort=trending) ::: :::{ .fragment } Transfer learning is the concept of taking a pre-trained model as a basis, then fine-tuning it to classify based on your own images. ::: ## How can CV be used in Healthcare? { .inverse } * classification * multi-classification * object detection * event detection ## How can CV be used in Healthcare? { .inverse }
::: { .incremental } * detecting disease or injury * monitoring patients vitals, e.g. respiratory rate * detecting bounds of a tumour when planning radiotherapy * automating cell counting * calculating the grade of a cancer * monitor for long-term changes, e.g. AAA * devices for patients with vision impairments * detecting when patients move (fall prevention) * monitoring the hygiene of a ward :::
## Issues with Computer Vision > ... one neural network learned to differentiate between dogs and wolves. It didn’t learn the > differences between dogs and wolves, but instead learned that wolves were on snow in their picture > and dogs were on grass. ::: footer [Dogs, Wolves, Data Science, and Why Machines Must Learn Like Humans Do (2017)](https://medium.com/veon-careers/dogs-wolves-data-science-and-why-machines-must-learn-like-humans-do-213b08036a10) ::: ## Issues with Computer Vision ![](https://www.science.org/do/10.1126/science.aay4197/full/ca_0621NID_Ai_Medical_Imaging_online-1644894873413.jpg) ::: footer [Artificial intelligence could revolutionize medical care. But don't trust it to read your x-ray just yet (2019)](https://www.science.org/content/article/artificial-intelligence-could-revolutionize-medical-care-don-t-trust-it-read-your-x-ray) ::: ## Issues with Computer Vision ::: {.fragment fragment-index=1} * algorithm trained at Mount Sinai Hospital, New York City * Busy ICU, many elderly patients * 34% of their x-rays came from patients with pneumonia * 93% accuracy ::: ::: {.fragment fragment-index=2} * tested at other sites, pneumonia ~1% of x-rays * accuracy dropped to 73%-80% ::: ::: footer [Artificial intelligence could revolutionize medical care. But don't trust it to read your x-ray just yet (2019)](https://www.science.org/content/article/artificial-intelligence-could-revolutionize-medical-care-don-t-trust-it-read-your-x-ray) ::: ## Issues with Computer Vision > At Mount Sinai, many of the infected patients were **too sick to get out of bed**, and so doctors > used a **portable chest x-ray machine**. Portable x-ray images **look very different** from those > created when a patient is standing up. Because of what it learned from Mount Sinai's x-rays, the > **algorithm began to associate a portable x-ray with illness**. It also **anticipated a high rate > of pneumonia**. ::: footer [Artificial intelligence could revolutionize medical care. But don't trust it to read your x-ray just yet (2019)](https://www.science.org/content/article/artificial-intelligence-could-revolutionize-medical-care-don-t-trust-it-read-your-x-ray) ::: ## The Unique Problems of Medical Computer Vision :::: { .columns } ::: {.column width=50%} ![](https://sironamedical.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/07/Screen-Shot-2022-01-04-at-3.44.56-PM.png) ::: ::: { .column width=50%} ::: { .medium } > This is the very unique problem of medical computer vision: we are attempting to solve a small > signal on the background of small noise whereas standard computer vision’s problem is a large > signal on the background of large noise. ::: ::: :::: ::: footer [The Unique Problems of Medical Computer Vision](https://sironamedical.com/the-unique-problems-of-medical-computer-vision/) ::: ## The Unique Problems of Medical Computer Vision :::: {.columns} :::{.column width=45%} :::{ .fragment fragment-index=1} :::{ .small } Is this a dog? ::: ![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/c/c4/Springer_Spaniel_Wide_Shot.jpg) ::: :::{ .fragment fragment-index=2} :::{ .small } [English Springer Spaniel in a Field, Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/English_Springer_Spaniel#/media/File:Springer_Spaniel_Wide_Shot.jpg) ::: ::: ::: :::{.column width=10%} ::: :::{.column width=45%} :::{ .fragment fragment-index=3} :::{ .small } [Small-cell carcinoma of the lung, Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Small-cell_carcinoma#/media/File:Lung_small_cell_carcinoma_(1)_by_core_needle_biopsy.jpg) ::: ![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/5/54/Lung_small_cell_carcinoma_%281%29_by_core_needle_biopsy.jpg) ::: ::: :::: :::{ .fragment fragment-index=4} :::{ .small } Annotations of cats & dogs is cheaper than reviewing medical scans/slides. The latter adds an additional burden on health systems. ::: ::: ## Other issues with CV :::{ .incremental } * Early detection vs over diagnosis * [Adversarial attacks](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2209.01962) can trick CV algorithms to incorrectly classify images * Computational power (environmental impact) * Governance: have we got consent to use images? * Explainability of Neural Networks :::

include-after-body:
<script>
  // htmlwidgets need to know to resize themselves when slides are shown/hidden.
  // Fire the "slideenter" event (handled by htmlwidgets.js) when the current
  // slide changes (different for each slide format).
  (function () {
    // dispatch for htmlwidgets
    function fireSlideEnter() {
      const event = window.document.createEvent("Event");
      event.initEvent("slideenter", true, true);
      window.document.dispatchEvent(event);
    }

    function fireSlideChanged(previousSlide, currentSlide) {
      fireSlideEnter();

      // dispatch for shiny
      if (window.jQuery) {
        if (previousSlide) {
          window.jQuery(previousSlide).trigger("hidden");
        }
        if (currentSlide) {
          window.jQuery(currentSlide).trigger("shown");
        }
      }
    }

    // hookup for slidy
    if (window.w3c_slidy) {
      window.w3c_slidy.add_observer(function (slide_num) {
        // slide_num starts at position 1
        fireSlideChanged(null, w3c_slidy.slides[slide_num - 1]);
      });
    }

  })();
</script>
